D6 Display L4 Analyze Reference

sRGB vs Display-P3 on the Web

color(display-p3 1 0 0) reaches a red that #ff0000 simply cannot describe — CSS Color 4 lets the web address the wider gamut

For decades web colour meant sRGB. CSS Color 4 adds wide-gamut syntax: color(display-p3 r g b) addresses the P3 space most modern screens cover. P3's red and green primaries sit well outside sRGB, so a P3 red is noticeably more saturated than the reddest #ff0000 — on a display that can show it. Older displays gracefully clamp.

Highlight a primary and see how far P3 extends past sRGB on the chromaticity chart. The shaded sliver is colour the web could not name before. On an sRGB screen the two swatches look alike; on a P3 screen the P3 one is visibly purer.
